title: Home resource service and media workflow families defined
objective: Define governed workflows for energy, gas, storage, water, food, irrigation, appliances, media, network, security, and maintenance over the verified home twin.
home_twin_basis: HOME-001 (parent) delivers the read-only verified home twin: every observed asset has identity, owner, telemetry provenance, simulation representation, privacy boundary, failure mode, and proof, and observation causes no physical mutation. HOME-002 builds governed WORKFLOWS on top of that already-proven twin and adds NO physical mutation of its own; it defines how each service reads, constrains, simulates, seeks approval, fails safe, is overridden, rolls back, and proves itself — while every physical/financial/utility/network effect stays human-gated (deferred to HOME-003 execution).
governing_constraint: NO AUTONOMOUS PHYSICAL ACTION. This is a LifeOS Product / Safety Agent family: every one of the eleven services observes, constrains, simulates, recommends, and proves, but never actuates a device, spends money, mutates a utility, changes the network, or accesses a credential on its own. blocked_paths for this task are explicit and total: physical control; purchase; utility mutation; network mutation; credential access; product implementation. Any real-world effect is executed only after an explicit human approval crossing a per-service impact threshold; this planning phase performs none. Workflow authority is a delegated, revocable SUBORDINATE of the LifeOS authority substrate — the single durable writer stays PostgreSQL + RuVector (STORE-001), and no home service becomes a second durable writer or an independent control plane.

shared_mechanisms.read_only_baseline: Baseline is drawn from the HOME-001 verified twin: high-frequency device telemetry is buffered transiently in redb (microsecond, in-process) and forward-flushed by envctl into the canonical durable twin state in PostgreSQL + RuVector (STORE-001 state-classes 1-3). Establishing a baseline reads only; it mutates no device and no network.
shared_mechanisms.constraints: Per-service safety, regulatory, comfort, and budget bounds are encoded as relational + knowledge-graph rows in PostgreSQL and enforced by Causal Graph Verification: a proposal whose claim lacks a historical root in the verified twin graph is rejected (anti-bluffing).
shared_mechanisms.simulation: Every workflow is simulated against the digital twin FIRST, never against reality. ATAS runs Echo-State-Network ensemble simulations and COW (Copy-On-Write) staging branches to forecast Future Timelines (state diffs, resource burn, failure nodes) up to N steps; a trajectory that 'spirals out of bounds' (unsafe) is caught by the Temporal Strange Attractor before any commit. This is 'simulate and commit', never 'guess and test'.
shared_mechanisms.approval_thresholds: RuvLTRA Proportional Intelligence routes by impact: reversible, low-consequence proposals clear a light approval tier; irreversible, safety-critical, spend, utility, or network proposals escalate above the threshold to MANDATORY explicit human approval (plus heavy cloud-model validation). The threshold is the impact boundary above which no action proceeds without human sign-off.
shared_mechanisms.safe_failure: On drift or hallucination, RuVector MinCut deterministically severs the offending workflow agent in subpolynomial time O(n^{o(1)}) without restarting the swarm; combined with fail-safe device defaults and redb acting as an application-level WAL so the edge node stays functional during PostgreSQL outage. On any uncertainty the workflow degrades to observe-only.
shared_mechanisms.manual_override: The human is always authoritative via LifeOS Mission Control over Unix Domain Sockets (reading the same memory-mapped redb state the agents write). Manual override is immediate and supersedes any agent proposal; agent authority is delegated and revocable (STORE-001 subordinate), never independent.
shared_mechanisms.rollback: COW discards any rejected or failed timeline (it is never merged to the main trunk, so there is no dirty-repository state); the task-level rollback is exactly the recorded plan — restore the archived home-service plan; no service or device state is changed.
shared_mechanisms.proof: Every recommendation or (human-approved, future) action is bound to a SHAKE256 Cryptographic Witness Chain rooted in a real twin source vector (telemetry), so the workflow cannot bluff a device state or dependency; Causal Graph Verification rejects any claim without a historical root, and a deterministic proof record is required before anything counts.
shared_mechanisms.no_autonomous_physical_action: The crux and the safety invariant. No service actuates hardware, spends, mutates a utility, or changes the network autonomously. blocked_paths (physical control, purchase, utility mutation, network mutation, credential access, product implementation) are total for this phase; every physical effect is human-gated and deferred to HOME-003.

work_items.surface: Household electrical energy workflow (grid draw, solar/PV generation, battery flow, per-circuit load, tariff/time-of-use) over the twin's resource + environment classes.
work_items.owner: LifeOS Product / Safety Agent (energy service is a governed subordinate; no control plane)
work_items.read_only_baseline: Reads per-circuit load, PV generation, battery flow, and grid draw telemetry from the HOME-001 twin (redb buffer -> envctl -> PostgreSQL durable history). Baseline establishes normal consumption/generation curves without touching any breaker or meter.
work_items.constraints: Electrical safety codes and circuit ampacity ratings, battery chemistry SoC window (e.g. 20-90%), inverter limits, and a monetary tariff/budget bound; all encoded in the PostgreSQL constraint graph and enforced by Causal Graph Verification.
work_items.simulation: Load-shift, PV-self-consumption, and peak-shaving plans are simulated against the twin via ATAS/COW; resource-burn and failure-node forecasts show cost and overload risk before any real switching is proposed.
work_items.approval_thresholds: Advisory reports (e.g. 'shift the dryer off peak') clear the light tier; any breaker/load switching, battery charge/discharge command, or tariff-plan change is above threshold and requires explicit human approval (breaker switching = physical control; tariff change = purchase/utility mutation, both blocked here).
work_items.safe_failure: On sensor drift or agent hallucination, MinCut severs the energy agent and the workflow reverts to observe-only; breakers and inverter settings are left untouched (fail in place).
work_items.manual_override: The human can veto or override any energy recommendation instantly from Mission Control; the physical breaker panel and inverter remain under human control at all times.
work_items.rollback: A rejected energy plan is discarded via COW and never merged; task rollback restores the archived home-service plan with no meter, breaker, battery, or tariff state changed.
work_items.proof: Each energy recommendation is witnessed by a SHAKE256 chain rooted in the twin's metered telemetry, so a claimed load or generation figure cannot be fabricated.
work_items.no_autonomous_physical_action: Never switches a breaker or load, never commands battery charge/discharge, never changes a tariff or utility account autonomously — all such effects are human-gated and out of scope for this phase.
work_items.home_twin_basis: HOME-001 resource + environment observation classes (energy telemetry with provenance).
work_items.blueprint_basis: Tiered memory (redb telemetry buffer -> PostgreSQL durable, Blueprint §1-2); ATAS Future Timelines resource-burn forecast (§4); RuvLTRA impact routing (§4); STORE-001 state-classes 1-3.

work_items.surface: Natural-gas / propane line workflow (flow, line pressure, combustion appliances, leak/CO detection) over the twin's resource + environment classes. Life-safety critical.
work_items.owner: LifeOS Product / Safety Agent (gas service is a governed subordinate; life-safety fail-safe posture)
work_items.read_only_baseline: Reads gas flow, line pressure, and leak/CO detector telemetry from the twin; baseline models normal combustion-appliance demand without actuating any valve or igniter.
work_items.constraints: Leak-concentration and CO thresholds, safe pressure envelope, and combustion-safety code limits; encoded in PostgreSQL and enforced by Causal Graph Verification. A detected-leak claim must trace to a real detector source vector.
work_items.simulation: Demand and pressure-drop scenarios are simulated on the twin; a trajectory crossing a leak/CO bound is flagged by the Temporal Strange Attractor as out-of-bounds before any recommendation.
work_items.approval_thresholds: Informational demand reports clear the light tier; ANY valve or ignition action is above threshold and permanently human-gated — the system may recommend a human shutoff but never crosses the physical-control boundary itself.
work_items.safe_failure: On a suspected leak or CO event the workflow raises an immediate safe alert and recommends human shutoff; it does NOT autonomously actuate the gas valve. Gas hardware fails in its physically safe position; the agent degrades to observe-and-alert only.
work_items.manual_override: The human (and the physical manual gas shutoff) is always authoritative; Mission Control surfaces the alert but the human executes any shutoff.
work_items.rollback: No gas state is ever changed by the workflow, so rollback is trivially the archived-plan restore; any rejected recommendation is discarded via COW.
work_items.proof: Every leak/CO alert is bound to a SHAKE256 witness chain rooted in the detector telemetry vector, preventing a bluffed or phantom alert as well as a suppressed real one.
work_items.no_autonomous_physical_action: Absolute: never opens/closes a gas valve, never ignites an appliance. This is the strictest instance of the family-wide no-autonomous-physical-action invariant.
work_items.home_twin_basis: HOME-001 resource + environment classes (gas flow, pressure, leak/CO detectors with provenance and failure mode).
work_items.blueprint_basis: Twin telemetry provenance (HOME-001); Temporal Strange Attractor out-of-bounds detection (§4); SHAKE256 Cryptographic Witness Chains (§6); STORE-001 forward-only telemetry.

work_items.surface: Household resource-reserve workflow (battery bank state-of-charge, water cistern/tank level, propane tank, pantry/consumable reserve) over the twin's resource class.
work_items.owner: LifeOS Product / Safety Agent (reserve service is a governed subordinate; no control plane)
work_items.read_only_baseline: Reads reserve levels (battery SoC, tank levels, consumable counts) from the twin; baseline models depletion and replenishment rates without commanding any charge/discharge or fill.
work_items.constraints: Per-reserve min/max levels and safe operating windows (battery SoC bounds, tank overfill/low limits, spoilage windows for consumables); PostgreSQL-encoded and Causal-Graph-verified.
work_items.simulation: Depletion-vs-demand and replenishment plans are simulated on the twin (e.g. 'battery reaches 15% by 20:00'); ATAS forecasts a reserve-exhaustion failure node before recommending action.
work_items.approval_thresholds: Reserve status and depletion forecasts clear the light tier; any battery charge/discharge command, tank fill, or reorder is above threshold and human-gated (charge/discharge = physical control; reorder = purchase, blocked here).
work_items.safe_failure: On drift, MinCut severs the reserve agent; no charge/discharge/fill command is issued and reserves hold at their current safe level.
work_items.manual_override: The human can override any reserve recommendation and retains direct control of chargers, valves, and reordering.
work_items.rollback: Rejected reserve plans are COW-discarded; task rollback restores the archived plan with no battery, tank, or inventory state changed.
work_items.proof: Reserve figures are witnessed by SHAKE256 chains rooted in the twin's level telemetry, so a claimed reserve level cannot be fabricated.
work_items.no_autonomous_physical_action: Never commands a charger, valve, or pump and never reorders a consumable autonomously.
work_items.home_twin_basis: HOME-001 resource observation class (reserve levels with provenance).
work_items.blueprint_basis: redb buffer role as a bounded high-frequency reserve tier vs PostgreSQL durable reserve history (Blueprint §1-2); ATAS failure-node forecasting (§4); STORE-001 state-classes 1-3.

work_items.surface: Household water workflow (main flow, per-fixture use, line pressure, leak detection, freeze risk, main/zone shutoff) over the twin's resource + environment classes. Life-safety / property-damage critical.
work_items.owner: LifeOS Product / Safety Agent (water service is a governed subordinate; fail-safe posture)
work_items.read_only_baseline: Reads main and fixture flow, pressure, leak-sensor, and freeze-risk telemetry from the twin; baseline models normal usage patterns without actuating any valve.
work_items.constraints: Leak/continuous-flow thresholds, pressure envelope, freeze-temperature bound, and municipal-use limits; PostgreSQL-encoded, Causal-Graph-verified.
work_items.simulation: Usage anomalies and leak scenarios are simulated on the twin; a continuous-flow or pressure-loss trajectory is caught as out-of-bounds before any recommendation.
work_items.approval_thresholds: Usage/leak reports clear the light tier; any main or zone valve actuation is above threshold and human-gated — the workflow recommends a human shutoff on a detected leak but never actuates the valve itself.
work_items.safe_failure: On a detected leak or freeze risk the workflow raises a safe alert and recommends human shutoff; it does not autonomously close the main valve. Valves fail in place; the agent degrades to observe-and-alert.
work_items.manual_override: The human and the physical main shutoff are always authoritative; Mission Control surfaces the alert for human action.
work_items.rollback: No water state is changed; rollback is the archived-plan restore, and rejected recommendations are COW-discarded.
work_items.proof: Leak and usage alerts are SHAKE256-witnessed against the twin's flow/pressure telemetry, preventing phantom or suppressed alerts.
work_items.no_autonomous_physical_action: Never opens or closes a water valve and never mutates the water utility account.
work_items.home_twin_basis: HOME-001 resource + environment classes (water flow, pressure, leak/freeze sensors with provenance and failure mode).
work_items.blueprint_basis: Twin telemetry provenance (HOME-001); Temporal Strange Attractor out-of-bounds detection (§4); SHAKE256 Witness Chains (§6); STORE-001 forward-only telemetry.

work_items.surface: Food-inventory workflow (pantry/fridge/freezer stock, expiry, dietary and allergen policy, depletion) over the twin's resource class, cross-linked to the personal substrate.
work_items.owner: LifeOS Product / Safety Agent + LifeOS Privacy Agent (food links to personal health data)
work_items.read_only_baseline: Reads food stock, expiry dates, and appliance (fridge/freezer) temperature telemetry from the twin; baseline models depletion and spoilage without reordering or actuating any appliance.
work_items.constraints: Expiry/spoilage windows, minimum pantry levels, and dietary/allergen policy inherited from the personal substrate (PERSONAL-002 health/privacy); PostgreSQL-encoded, Causal-Graph-verified, privacy boundary preserved.
work_items.simulation: Meal-plan and depletion scenarios are simulated on the twin; ATAS forecasts a stock-out failure node before recommending a shopping list.
work_items.approval_thresholds: Inventory lists and expiry warnings clear the light tier; any reorder/purchase or appliance state change is above threshold and human-gated (purchase is blocked in this phase).
work_items.safe_failure: On drift, MinCut severs the food agent; the workflow reverts to observe-only and no reorder or appliance command is issued. Fridge/freezer fail at their safe (running) default.
work_items.manual_override: The human approves or edits any shopping list and retains full control of purchasing and appliances; dietary/allergen policy is human-owned.
work_items.rollback: Rejected lists are COW-discarded; rollback restores the archived plan with no purchase made and no appliance changed.
work_items.proof: Inventory and expiry claims are SHAKE256-witnessed against the twin's stock/temperature telemetry; dietary constraints trace to the personal substrate provenance.
work_items.no_autonomous_physical_action: Never places an order and never actuates a refrigeration appliance autonomously; personal dietary data never leaves its boundary silently (PERSONAL-001 boundary honored).
work_items.home_twin_basis: HOME-001 resource + appliance classes (food stock, fridge/freezer telemetry); personal substrate for dietary policy.
work_items.blueprint_basis: ATAS stock-out failure-node forecasting (§4); Causal Graph Verification (§6); STORE-001 telemetry tiering; PERSONAL-001/-002 privacy-boundary inheritance (personal substrate input).

work_items.surface: Outdoor irrigation workflow (zone schedules, soil-moisture, weather forecast, pump/zone valves, municipal restrictions) over the twin's resource + environment classes.
work_items.owner: LifeOS Product / Safety Agent (irrigation service is a governed subordinate; no control plane)
work_items.read_only_baseline: Reads current schedules, soil-moisture, flow, and weather telemetry from the twin; baseline models watering demand without opening any zone valve or starting any pump.
work_items.constraints: Per-zone runtime limits, soil-moisture targets, municipal watering-day/drought restrictions, and freeze cutoff; PostgreSQL-encoded, Causal-Graph-verified.
work_items.simulation: Proposed watering schedules are simulated on the twin against weather and soil-moisture forecasts; ATAS resource-burn shows water cost and over/under-watering risk before any schedule change is proposed.
work_items.approval_thresholds: Schedule recommendations clear the light tier; any zone-valve actuation, pump start, or committed schedule change is above threshold and human-gated (valve/pump = physical control, blocked here).
work_items.safe_failure: On drift, MinCut severs the irrigation agent; the current schedule holds and no valve/pump command is issued. Valves fail closed (no unintended watering).
work_items.manual_override: The human approves any schedule change and retains direct control of the irrigation controller.
work_items.rollback: Rejected schedules are COW-discarded; rollback restores the archived plan with no valve, pump, or committed schedule changed.
work_items.proof: Watering recommendations are SHAKE256-witnessed against soil-moisture and weather telemetry vectors.
work_items.no_autonomous_physical_action: Never opens a zone valve, starts a pump, or commits a schedule change autonomously.
work_items.home_twin_basis: HOME-001 resource + environment classes (irrigation schedules, soil-moisture, weather with provenance).
work_items.blueprint_basis: ATAS Future Timelines resource-burn/failure forecasting (§4); RuvLTRA impact routing (§4); STORE-001 telemetry tiering.

work_items.surface: Household appliance workflow (HVAC/thermostat, washer/dryer, oven/range, water heater, smart appliances) over the twin's appliance + environment classes.
work_items.owner: LifeOS Product / Safety Agent (appliance service is a governed subordinate; no control plane)
work_items.read_only_baseline: Reads appliance run-state, cycle, energy draw, and thermostat/comfort telemetry from the twin; baseline models normal duty cycles without actuating any appliance.
work_items.constraints: Manufacturer safe operating ranges, thermostat comfort/safety bounds (e.g. anti-freeze / max-temp limits), duty-cycle limits, and energy budget; PostgreSQL-encoded, Causal-Graph-verified.
work_items.simulation: Setpoint and scheduling changes (e.g. HVAC pre-cool, deferred wash cycle) are simulated on the twin; ATAS forecasts comfort, energy burn, and failure nodes before any state change is proposed.
work_items.approval_thresholds: Comfort/energy advice clears the light tier; any appliance state change (setpoint write, cycle start/stop, mode change) is above threshold and human-gated (state change = physical control, blocked here).
work_items.safe_failure: On drift, MinCut severs the appliance agent; the thermostat holds a safe default and appliances remain in their current or idle-safe state. The workflow reverts to observe-only.
work_items.manual_override: The human can override any appliance recommendation instantly; physical controls and the thermostat remain human-authoritative.
work_items.rollback: Rejected appliance plans are COW-discarded; rollback restores the archived plan with no setpoint or appliance state changed.
work_items.proof: Appliance recommendations are SHAKE256-witnessed against the twin's run-state and energy telemetry, preventing a bluffed appliance state.
work_items.no_autonomous_physical_action: Never writes a setpoint, starts/stops a cycle, or changes an appliance mode autonomously.
work_items.home_twin_basis: HOME-001 appliance + environment observation classes (appliance run-state, thermostat, energy with provenance).
work_items.blueprint_basis: ATAS Future Timelines (state diffs, resource burn, failure nodes, §4); RuvLTRA impact routing (§4); STORE-001 telemetry tiering.

work_items.surface: Home media workflow (playback devices, library/catalog, quiet-hours, bandwidth, family/parental policy) over the twin's media class, cross-linked to the LifeOS media workspace and personal knowledge.
work_items.owner: LifeOS Product / Safety Agent + LifeOS Privacy Agent (media links to family/personal policy)
work_items.read_only_baseline: Reads media-device state, library metadata, and bandwidth telemetry from the twin; baseline models normal playback/usage without changing any playback or purchasing content.
work_items.constraints: Content and family/parental policy, quiet-hours windows, and bandwidth/QoS budget; PostgreSQL-encoded, Causal-Graph-verified, privacy boundary preserved.
work_items.simulation: Playlist/scene and bandwidth-scheduling proposals are simulated on the twin; resource-burn shows bandwidth impact before any change is proposed.
work_items.approval_thresholds: Recommendations (e.g. 'queue this for movie night') clear the light tier; any autonomous playback change or content purchase is above threshold and human-gated (purchase blocked here).
work_items.safe_failure: On drift, MinCut severs the media agent; current playback is untouched and the workflow reverts to observe-only.
work_items.manual_override: The human approves any playback/scene change; family/parental policy is human-owned and overrides any agent proposal.
work_items.rollback: Rejected media plans are COW-discarded; rollback restores the archived plan with no playback or purchase changed.
work_items.proof: Media recommendations are SHAKE256-witnessed against device/library telemetry; content/parental constraints trace to the personal-knowledge provenance.
work_items.no_autonomous_physical_action: Never changes playback state or purchases content autonomously; family viewing/listening data stays within its privacy boundary.
work_items.home_twin_basis: HOME-001 media observation class (media devices, library with provenance); LifeOS media workspace + personal knowledge substrate.
work_items.blueprint_basis: LifeOS UDS media/status rendering ('reading the same memory-mapped redb file'); ATAS resource-burn forecast (§4); PERSONAL-001 privacy-boundary inheritance.

work_items.surface: Home network workflow (topology, devices, bandwidth/QoS, security posture, DNS/DHCP/firewall configuration) over the twin's network class. Strictly read-only in this family.
work_items.owner: LifeOS Product / Safety Agent (network service is a governed subordinate; read-only, no control plane)
work_items.read_only_baseline: Reads network topology, connected devices, bandwidth, and security-posture telemetry from the twin; baseline maps the network without altering any router, firewall, DNS, or DHCP setting.
work_items.constraints: Topology invariants, segmentation/security policy, and bandwidth/QoS budgets; PostgreSQL-encoded, Causal-Graph-verified. A device or route claim must trace to a real twin source vector.
work_items.simulation: Segmentation, QoS, and firewall-rule proposals are simulated on the twin twin-graph via COW; MinCut-style dependency analysis shows blast radius before any change is proposed.
work_items.approval_thresholds: Topology maps and posture reports clear the light tier; ANY network configuration change (firewall, router, DNS, DHCP, segmentation) is above threshold and permanently human-gated — network mutation is an explicit blocked_path for this phase.
work_items.safe_failure: On drift, MinCut severs the network agent; no configuration is touched and the workflow reverts to read-only topology observation.
work_items.manual_override: The human is always authoritative over network configuration; Mission Control surfaces recommendations only.
work_items.rollback: No network state is ever changed; rollback is the archived-plan restore, and rejected recommendations are COW-discarded.
work_items.proof: Topology and posture findings are SHAKE256-witnessed against the twin's network telemetry, so a claimed device or route cannot be fabricated.
work_items.no_autonomous_physical_action: Never mutates the network — no firewall, router, DNS, DHCP, or segmentation change is made autonomously (network mutation blocked_path).
work_items.home_twin_basis: HOME-001 network observation class (topology, devices, posture with provenance and privacy boundary).
work_items.blueprint_basis: RuVector MinCut dependency-graph blast-radius reasoning (§6); Causal Graph Verification (§6); STORE-001 no-second-durable-writer; task blocked_paths (network mutation).

work_items.surface: Home security workflow (door/window locks, cameras, alarm/arming, access events, life-safety egress) over the twin's environment + appliance classes. Highest-consequence service.
work_items.owner: LifeOS Product / Safety Agent (security service is a governed subordinate; life-safety fail-safe/fail-secure posture)
work_items.read_only_baseline: Reads lock state, camera/sensor status, alarm state, and access-event telemetry from the twin; baseline models normal occupancy and access without actuating any lock or alarm and while honoring camera privacy boundaries.
work_items.constraints: Life-safety code (egress must fail-SAFE / unlock on fire or alarm; perimeter fail-SECURE), camera privacy boundaries, and access policy; PostgreSQL-encoded, Causal-Graph-verified.
work_items.simulation: Arming schedules and access-policy proposals are simulated on the twin; a trajectory that would leave an egress locked during an alarm or a perimeter unlocked is caught as out-of-bounds before any recommendation.
work_items.approval_thresholds: Occupancy/status and anomaly reports clear the light tier; ANY lock/unlock, arm/disarm, or access-grant is above threshold and permanently human-gated (physical control, blocked here). Life-safety egress fail-safe is a code-mandated hardware behavior, not an autonomous agent action.
work_items.safe_failure: Hardware fails per life-safety code (egress unlocks on alarm; perimeter holds secure); on agent drift, MinCut severs the security agent and the workflow degrades to observe-and-alert. The system never autonomously unlocks a perimeter door.
work_items.manual_override: The human and physical keys/manual controls are always authoritative; Mission Control surfaces alerts, the human executes any lock/arm change.
work_items.rollback: No lock/alarm state is changed by the workflow; rollback is the archived-plan restore, and rejected recommendations are COW-discarded.
work_items.proof: Access and alarm findings are SHAKE256-witnessed against the twin's sensor telemetry, preventing a bluffed unlock event or a suppressed real one.
work_items.no_autonomous_physical_action: Absolute: never locks/unlocks a door, arms/disarms an alarm, or grants access autonomously — the highest-stakes instance of the family-wide invariant.
work_items.home_twin_basis: HOME-001 environment + appliance classes (locks, cameras, alarm, access events with provenance, failure mode, and privacy boundary).
work_items.blueprint_basis: Twin telemetry provenance + privacy boundary (HOME-001); Temporal Strange Attractor out-of-bounds detection (§4); MinCut deterministic isolation (§6); SHAKE256 Witness Chains (§6).

work_items.surface: Predictive maintenance workflow (failure-node forecasting across appliances/HVAC/pumps/systems, service intervals, technician dispatch) over the whole twin.
work_items.owner: LifeOS Product / Safety Agent (maintenance service is a governed subordinate; recommend-only)
work_items.read_only_baseline: Reads cross-system health telemetry (runtime hours, vibration/temperature trends, error codes) from the twin; baseline models each system's normal wear curve without scheduling or performing any repair.
work_items.constraints: Manufacturer service intervals, failure-prediction thresholds, and a maintenance budget; PostgreSQL-encoded, Causal-Graph-verified.
work_items.simulation: ATAS runs failure-node forecasting over the twin's telemetry (predicting e.g. a compressor or pump degradation before failure); Temporal Strange Attractors identify systems spiraling toward failure before any dispatch is proposed.
work_items.approval_thresholds: Predictive alerts and service recommendations clear the light tier; any technician dispatch, parts order, or scheduling commitment is above threshold and human-gated (purchase/dispatch blocked here).
work_items.safe_failure: On drift, MinCut severs the maintenance agent; no dispatch or order is issued and the workflow reverts to observe-and-forecast only.
work_items.manual_override: The human approves any service action and owns all scheduling and spend.
work_items.rollback: Rejected maintenance plans are COW-discarded; rollback restores the archived plan with no dispatch made and no service state changed.
work_items.proof: Every failure-node prediction is SHAKE256-witnessed against the underlying telemetry trend vector, so a predicted failure cannot be bluffed and a real one cannot be silently dropped.
work_items.no_autonomous_physical_action: Never dispatches a technician, orders parts, or performs a repair autonomously.
work_items.home_twin_basis: HOME-001 appliance + resource + environment classes (cross-system health telemetry with provenance and failure mode).
work_items.blueprint_basis: ATAS failure-node forecasting + Temporal Strange Attractors (§4, 'predict ... before writing any actual production code' applied to physical systems); RuvLTRA impact routing (§4); STORE-001 telemetry tiering.

inherited_invariants: Single durable writer: the durable home-twin macro-state (asset identity, history, provenance, permissions, constraint graph) is owned by PostgreSQL + RuVector; no home service becomes a second durable writer.
Forward-only sync: high-frequency device telemetry buffers in redb and is forward-flushed by envctl into PostgreSQL; no undirected redb<->PostgreSQL dual-write.
No sidecar: semantic twin queries (dependent devices, causal edges) run inside PostgreSQL via the native RuVector extension; the home runs no autonomous external vector database.
Provenance preserved: every telemetry-derived recommendation keeps a SHAKE256 witness lineage rooted in a real twin source vector.
Delegated, revocable authority: each service workflow is a subordinate of the LifeOS authority substrate, never an independent control plane.
store_001_alignment.consequence_for_this_family: Home service workflows read, constrain, simulate, recommend, and prove, then defer every physical/financial/utility/network effect to human approval; they introduce no new durable authority and add no physical mutation to the STORE-001 authority model.

execution_order_rationale: Every service establishes its read-only baseline first regardless of order. Decomposition proceeds one service at a time (per next_action) from the lowest-actuation-risk / read-only-dominant services (network read-only, media, maintenance-forecast) toward the highest-consequence life-safety actuation contracts (water, gas, security), so the strictest fail-safe boundaries receive the most scrutiny and are settled last.
open_dependencies: HOME-001 verified home twin must be proven first; its task family and proof are not yet produced, so this family is defined against the HOME-001 CSV contract (goal + gate) and inherits the twin when HOME-001 completes.
Live physical actuation, purchase, utility mutation, and network mutation are all deferred to HOME-003 (human-gated execution) and are out of scope here.
Live RuVector-in-PostgreSQL extension activation not yet locally proven (STORE-001 open dependency); semantic twin queries fall back to the proven Bun-loaded ruvector N-API path until then.
Food and media dietary/family/parental policy inherits the personal substrate (PERSONAL-001/-002); those privacy boundaries must hold when the personal family is proven.
